article_title: T1 Energy to Buy KORE Power for $32 Million, Entering Battery Storage and AI Data Center Markets

content_preview.title: T1 Energy to Buy KORE Power for $32 Million, Entering Battery Storage and AI Data Center Markets
content_preview.summary: T1 Energy, the former FREYR, agreed to acquire battery storage provider KORE Power for about $32 million, adding 1,100 deployed projects.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

T1 Energy has agreed to acquire battery energy storage provider KORE Power, a deal the U.S. solar and battery company said would give it an entry point into the energy storage and AI data center infrastructure markets, according to [T1 Energy](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/06/03/3305851/0/en/t1-energy-to-enter-bess-and-data-center-infrastructure-markets-with-acquisition-of-kore-power.html). The purchase enterprise value consists of approximately $32 million of equi...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured M&A News piece (612 words) on the T1 Energy / KORE Power deal, careful to label projected figures as expectations rather than results.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (GlobeNewswire, T1 release) confirms the ~$32 million enterprise value, the ~1,100 BESS projects, the 'over 50 years' track record, the $9.6 million earn-out, the '$15 million to $20 million of EBITDA in 2027', the 45 GWh to 143 GWh (2035) market projection, the Q2-2026 close, the Dan Barcelo 'We're excited to welcome the NRI team' quote, and the Jay Bellows 'one-stop solution' quote. source-1 (Energy-Storage.News) confirms the former-FREYR / Norway origin, the NRI BESS division, the T1 NRI rebrand, and the Barcelo 'utility scale developers' quote. source-2 (Solar Power World) confirms the Dallas / Austin facilities. source-3 (StockTitan) confirms the NYSE 'TE' ticker and the AI-data-center framing.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every dollar figure, the project count, the GWh projections, and all three quotes are verbatim and correctly attributed (Barcelo's two quotes split between GlobeNewswire and Energy-Storage.News). 'Q2 2026' is rendered as 'the second quarter of 2026' (expansion only). The article appropriately flags in 'What We Don't Know' that the EBITDA and earn-out are projections and that the equity/cash/debt split of the $32M was not broken out.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; clearly distinguishes company projections from finalized results.
editor_notes.originality: New event — no prior T1 Energy / FREYR / KORE Power coverage in the archive.
recommendations: Added energy-storage.news and solarpowerworldonline.com to the allowlist (established energy-storage and solar trade publications).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified M&A submission with disciplined projection labeling. APPROVE.
